qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Authenticated dynamic node parameter resolution allows arbitrary server-side HTTP requests
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is an authenticated SSRF in `POST /rest/dynamic-node-parameters/options` via attacker-controlled `loadOptions.routing.request` merged into `RoutingNode` request options, while the advisory fixes webhook HTML-response CSP enforcement in `packages/core/src/html-sandbox.ts` for malformed `Content-Type` values.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's webhook-response CSP sandbox bug. The advisory patch trims `Content-Type` before HTML detection in `packages/core/src/html-sandbox.ts`, whereas the session's qualifying finding is a separate SSRF in dynamic node parameter resolution.
evidence: GHSA-825q-w924-xhgx describes stored XSS caused by webhook responses failing to receive the intended sandbox CSP when HTML `Content-Type` handling is incorrect.

Advisory fix commits `ced34c0f93ab4c759a56065965986094d8ef7323` and `e8cf4d6bb3af94dc296cbb67bc3dd20e9b508ac9` change `packages/core/src/html-sandbox.ts` from `contentType.toLowerCase()` to `contentType.trim().toLowerCase()` and add tests for leading/trailing spaces.

The only final `qualifying_vuln` finding in `.judge/input_manifest.json` is `dynamic_node_parameters_ssrf`, which targets `POST /rest/dynamic-node-parameters/options` and `packages/core/src/execution-engine/routing-node.ts`, not webhook response rendering or CSP enforcement.
